CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, old_column_name VARCHAR(100));
ALTER TABLE test_table RENAME COLUMN old_column_name TO new_column_name;
CREATE VIEW test_view AS SELECT new_column_name FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE dependent_table (dep_id INT, test_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (test_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
ALTER TABLE test_table RENAME COLUMN id TO identifier;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN additional_column VARCHAR(50);
ALTER TABLE test_table RENAME COLUMN additional_column TO extra_column;
CREATE FUNCTION test_function() RETURNS VOID AS $$ BEGIN RETURN; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
CREATE TABLE another_table (column_a INT, column_b VARCHAR(50), FOREIGN KEY (column_b) REFERENCES dependent_table(dep_id));
ALTER TABLE another_table RENAME COLUMN column_b TO renamed_column;
ALTER TABLE test_table RENAME COLUMN new_column_name TO renamed_new_column IF EXISTS;
